revision.text:
[[ja:忌部神社 (松江市)]] {{Expand Japanese|忌部神社 (松江市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Inbe Shrine (Matsue City) | native_name = 忌部神社 (松江市) | image = 忌部神社.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Inbe Shrine (Matsue City) | coordinates = {{coord|35.399028|133.03111}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Futodama|lt=Futodama|en|Futodama|it|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|ru|Футодама|simple|Futodama|zh|布刀玉命|qid=Q11059539|1=Futodama}} }} {{nihongo|'''Inbe Shrine (Matsue City)'''|忌部神社 (松江市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|gōshi|lt=gōshi|ja|合祀|qid=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Izumo Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumo Province|ja|出雲国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11395853|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumo Province}} ** Series ordinal: 17 ** P155: {{ill|Q135040776|ltq=Q135040776|qid=Q135040776|1=Fushifuno Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Tamatsukuriyu Shrine|lt=Tamatsukuriyu Shrine|ja|玉作湯神社|qid=Q11572397|1=Tamatsukuri Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q207321|ltq=Q207321|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}} * {{ill|Q582846|ltq=Q582846|qid=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}} * {{ill|Ou district|lt=Ou district|ja|意宇郡|zh|意宇郡|qid=Q11073594|1=Ou district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.399028|133.03111}} == image (P18) == * 忌部神社.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q11059539|ltq=Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|1=Futodama}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182791 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1280005000693 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Kutamino Shrine|lt=Kutamino Shrine|qid=Q135040777|1=Kutamino Shrine (Q135040777)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県松江市東忌部町957', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '島根県松江市東忌部町3000', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:忌部神社 (松江市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11490726}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Inbe Shrine |画像=[[File:忌部神社.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}} Higashi-Inbe-chō 957 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = October 19 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Inbe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Inbe Shrine''' (いんべじんじゃ) is a shrine in Higashi-Inbe-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. Until the end of Meiji, it was called Ōmiya Shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its main deity is {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}. As associated deities, it enshrines {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} (said to have been invited from {{ill|lt=Hinomisaki Shrine|Q1619446|qid=Q1619446|1=Hinomisaki Shrine}}) and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (said to have been invited from {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|qid=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}}), along with 22 other deities. === History === The area of the shrine’s location belonged to Inbe Kamube recorded in the Ōu District section of the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|qid=Q11395858|1=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki}}''{{Sfn|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|p=27}}. According to old records preserved at this shrine, it was once called Sansha Ōmiya Daimyōjin and was the central shrine of the Inbe area, and from around the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it came to be called Sōsha Ōmiya Sansho Daimyōjin and served as the general tutelary shrine of the district. The same region also had the Shikinaisha Kutami Shrine, which is considered to have become a branch shrine of Ōmiya in the medieval period{{Sfn|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|p=32}}. Under the {{ill|lt=shrine merger|Q11590700|qid=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} policy of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 44 ({{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|qid=Q2076|1=1911}}), Ōmiya Shrine merged village shrine {{ill|lt=Kutami Shrine (Matsue)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kutami Shrine}}, and unranked Suga Shrine, Nanatsugi Shrine, and Yamachi Shrine, and was newly established as village shrine Inbe Shrine{{Efn|Kutami Shrine was later re-established when ujiko invited a divided spirit to the pre-merger building in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|qid=Q2169|1=1923}}), and worship has continued there{{Sfn|Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places|p=32}}.}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|qid=Q18782|1=1931}}), it was promoted to gōsha. === Cultural properties === {{ill|lt=Magatama|Q334232|qid=Q334232|1=Magatama}}, {{ill|lt=kudatama|Q11603258|qid=Q11603258|1=Kudatama}}, and whetstones used in their production - thought to have been made in Inbe Kamube - are dedicated at the shrine. In Shōwa 39 ({{ill|lt=1964|Q2652|qid=Q2652|1=1964}}), these were designated tangible cultural properties by Matsue as “Ancient relics related to bead production”{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.matsue.shimane.jp/jumin/shisei/kouhou/kouhou_matsue/1508/1508d.htm|title=Kōhō Matsue No.625: Series Everyone’s Cultural Properties (4)|publisher={{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}}|date=2003-08|url-status=dead|url-status-date=2017-10|archiveurl=https://archive.fo/20120722123029/http://www.city.matsue.shimane.jp/jumin/shisei/kouhou/kouhou_matsue/1508/1508d.htm|archivedate=2012-07-22}}. === Notes and sources === === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === *{{cite book|和書|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.)|title=Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in|edition=New reprint|year=2000|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9|ref=Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Inbe clan|Q8189229|qid=Q8189229|1=Inbe clan}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Inbe Shrine (Matsue City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Inbe Shrine (Matsue City)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Inbe clan]]
